label: yes
cot_prefix: Okay, let's try to figure out this problem step by step. So, Jane was born on the last day of February in 2001. Today is her 16th birthday. We need to find the date one year ago from today in MM/DD/YYYY format. The options are given, so maybe checking each one could help, but let's think through it first. First, the last day of February. Normally, February has 28 days, but in a leap year, it has 29. Leap years are every 4 years, right? So 2001 isn't a leap year because 2000 was a leap year, then the next would be 2004. So, 2001's February had 28 days. Therefore, Jane was born on 2/28/2001. Today is her 16th birthday. That would mean today's date is 2/28/2017, because 2001 + 16 = 2017. Wait, but wait, let me check. If she was born in 2001, then adding 16 years would make her birthday in 2017. So today's date is 2/28/2017. Now the question is: What is the date one year ago from today? So if today is 2/28/2017, one year ago would be 2/28/2016. But wait, 2016 is a leap year, right? Because 2016 is divisible by 4 (2016 ÷ 4 = 504), so yes, it's a leap year. So February 2016 had 29 days. But the question isn't about Jane's birthday in 2016, it's about the date one year ago from today (which is 2/28/2017). So one year before that is 2/28/2016. Since that is a valid date (February 28 exists in any year), the answer would be 02/28/2016. Let me check the options. Option C is 02/28/2016. So that's probably the answer. But wait, let me make sure there isn't some trick here. Sometimes when people say "one year ago," they might refer to the same day of the year, but depending on the calendar. But in terms of exact one year, it's just subtracting 1 from the year. For example, if today is 2/28/2017, then one year ago is 2/28/2016. Even though in 2016, February had 29 days, the 28th still exists. So the date would be 02/28/2016. So option C. Let me check the other options. A is 02/28/2015. That would be two years ago from today (if today is 2/28/2017).
